claim: Kernel-expressibility coverage of the pinned TinyStories task-family corpus (1,709,765 tokens, corpus pin b6bcc9f6) is 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0, 0.2210 at rung kernel-v0, 0.7841 at the wn31-aligned band — measured 2026-07-08 on the INCOMPLETE kernel-v0 + molecules-v0 instance as it existed that day. The number is corpus-indexed, rung-indexed, and kernel-state-indexed: per the verdict's extrapolation envelope it extrapolates to NO other corpus, rung, or kernel state, and is re-measured as the kernel grows.
backing_ref: registry/verdicts/m0b.json sha256 da475e98744ffe1dc635d2aa814c1955a120cef6b93dedef771af9d087fb7f0d; analysis-output sha256 63ce6485; audit CONFIRMED registry/audits/m0b/1-gate-a-codex.json
notes: CORRECTION ENTRY: this claim had been narrated in programme discussion as the kernel's 'natural coverage' (a general property). That framing strips all three indices and was an unlicensed EXTRAPOLATION stated as fact — the motivating incident for this register (docs/next/assumption-register.md section 7). Doc-level fixes applied 2026-07-09 in docs/next/arch-survey.md and docs/next/00-programme-2-overview.md.

claim: A coverage level of roughly 0.35 would also hold on corpora other than the pinned TinyStories task family (e.g. the D-EXT external slice, general web text, or any later eval corpus).
resolution_path: Re-run the m0b census machinery (tools/experiments/m0b_instrument.py, deterministic, ~$0 on-box) on each new corpus BEFORE any design cites a coverage number for it or powers a covered-slice test on it. Live warning that this projection is doubtful: the f2 external slice showed zero item flips on D-EXT (registry/verdicts/f2.json sha256 38422b3c...) — in-house-slice behaviour did not transfer, and D-EXT lemma coverage is an open diagnosis question in the F2 post-mortem.
notes: Filed as the open counterpart of ASM-0001: the exact projection the 'natural coverage' narration silently asserted. May motivate queueing a census run; may not premise any decision while open.

claim: TinyStories task-family content-word mass is an adequate Tier-0 proxy for eval-relevant content when evaluating the NICHE-SCOPE coverage gate (pre-declared default X=0.20).
rationale: Ratified at GNG-0 as the pre-declared gate corpus for the R0/R1 rungs: cheap, pinned, deterministic census substrate matched to the small-model task family in use. Revisit condition: any experiment whose eval corpus is not TinyStories-derived must gate on a census of ITS OWN corpus (see ASM-0002 resolution path), and the proxy stipulation lapses for frontier-scale task families.
notes: Registered retroactively so the coverage gate's premise is visible as a stipulation rather than passing as established fact.

claim: The L3a axiom/world layers read certain minted concept URNs as RELATIONS with a stipulated direction: molecules-v0 'mother' as the functional relation forward=(child -> mother) (the maintainer's mother(Elvis)=Gladys reading), 'father' likewise; kernel-v0 'maker-of' as forward=(maker -> artifact); kernel-v0 'part-of'/'has-part' as a mutually inverse pair. These concepts' explications are role/kind scenarios, not natively relational schemas; the relational reading is an alias stipulation of the L3a corpora, not a property of the concepts.
rationale: v0 has no natively minted relational concepts for the family litmus (has-parent/has-mother do not exist; design-constraint-layer.md section 1.4). Stipulating the reading lets the engine leg run today on real minted URNs. Revisit condition: when relational concepts (has-parent, has-mother, person) are minted natively, re-author axioms-v0/world-v0 against them and re-run as a successor id; the stipulation then lapses.

claim: The Presley anchor records in data/world-v0 (mother(elvis-presley)=gladys-presley, father=vernon-presley, lisa-marie's parents) are accurate public facts. They are AGENT-RECALLED, not extracted from a pinned external source, and are provenance-stamped 'public-fact-agent-recalled'.
rationale: The l3a verdict scores answers AGAINST THE RECORDS, so external accuracy of the anchor facts is not load-bearing for the verdict; the anchor exists so the maintainer's worked case is a literal eval item (q0001). Revisit condition: any experiment that scores against reality (not records) must re-source these facts via a provenance-pinned extraction route (FK-L3-2).

claim: The l3a-eval expected answers, though computed from the generator's construction tables through a code path separate from the engine's licensing/index pipeline, share an author and a repo with the engine; agreement between engine and eval is therefore evidence of internal consistency, and the strict refusal-code semantics is where the substantive test lives. Residual single-author circularity remains.
rationale: Tier-0 instrument-validation experiments in this programme accept generator-vs-implementation separation plus the cross-vendor audit as the independence mechanism (directives section 8). Revisit condition: the l3a audit (Codex/GPT-5.5) must specifically re-derive a sample of expected answers from data/world-v0 records independently; if it cannot, the PASS does not confirm.

claim: The A5 construct->concept mapping table is a stipulated modelling choice: extracted Python constructs are asserted classes of the minted code-v0 concepts (python-module, python-function, python-class) and edges are asserted under the minted code-v0 relation concepts (code-calls, code-defines, code-imports) plus the REUSED kernel-v0 part-of/has-part pair read as transitive lexical containment. Every mapping is by EXACT minted content-hash URN (X3 trap honoured); the adequacy of these six structural definitions as kernel concepts is stipulated, not evidenced, and the code-v0 records are structural definitions, NOT kot-ast/1 NSM explications.
rationale: v0 has no code-construct concepts; minting six structural-definition concepts through the canonical mint tool (content-addressed, corpus-specific profile kot-code-construct/1, precedent math-v0/pm-ast/1) lets the code vertical run today with exact concept identity. Revisit condition: when the idea-A sub-programme mints NSM-explicated code concepts, re-author code-axioms-v0/code-world-v0 against them and re-run as a successor id; the stipulation then lapses.

claim: The a5-eval expected answers, though computed from the generator's own construction tables (tools/axiom/gen_a5_corpora.py, which never imports the engine or the code query layer), share an author and a repo with the engine; agreement between engine and eval is therefore evidence of internal consistency, and the strict refusal-code semantics is where the substantive test lives. Residual single-author circularity remains.
rationale: Same independence mechanism as l3a/ASM-0006: generator-vs-implementation code-path separation plus the cross-vendor audit. Revisit condition: the a5 audit (Codex/GPT-5.5, run-vs-audit separation) must re-derive a sample of expected answers from data/code-corpus-v0 sources and data/code-world-v0 records independently; if it cannot, the PASS does not confirm.

claim: The extracted code-world-v0 records are the kot-code-extract/1 extractor's CONSERVATIVE STATIC assertions about the pinned snapshot corpus (exact-name call resolution only; corpus-internal imports only; immediate-child defines edges only; unresolvable constructs assert nothing). Their soundness/completeness with respect to Python RUNTIME semantics (dynamic dispatch, shadowing, monkey-patching) is not claimed and is not load-bearing: the a5 verdict scores answers AGAINST THE RECORDS.
rationale: Mirror of ASM-0005 (against-records scoring). Revisit condition: any successor that scores against ground-truth program behaviour (not records) must re-derive the graph with a soundness-validated extractor and register its own extraction-adequacy evidence.

claim: The f7 capstone's default reuse accounting — consuming each survivor's already-logged R1–R3 cells as Case-B comparator cells for the slope fit, paying GPU only for added rungs (R4/T*) plus one fresh full-arm overlap cell at R3 as the RC-5 reuse-consistency instrument — preserves the capstone's evidential value relative to a full re-run.
rationale: Design default set by docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md §3.4-3. The slope estimand and margins are fixed in the DRAFT before added-rung data exists; the RC-5 overlap cell converts environment-equality from assumption to measurement. Revisit condition: gate-t5 dry-plan, where BOTH accountings (full re-run vs reuse) are disclosed and the maintainer ratifies one; a failed RC-5 overlap bound voids this stipulation and forces fresh lower-rung runs. Registered by the Fable Designer role (bead kernel-of-truth-utq).

claim: Folding reuse-maximising ordering INTO the frozen backlog score (mechanically derived L from registry/components.jsonl consumers + registry/artifact-ledger.jsonl, plus a producers-before-consumers tie-break) encodes the reuse priority at least as well as a standing separate ordering would, without creating a second prioritisation surface that could be cited selectively.
rationale: Design choice set by docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md §4.4 amending research-engine §2.7. Revisit condition: the first generation boundary after the D3 backlog scorer lands — if re-scoring fails to reproduce the plan's §4.2 wave ordering, the discrepancy is a recorded finding to resolve there (companion to fork ENG-2). Registered by the Fable Designer role (bead kernel-of-truth-utq).

claim: REVISION-1 REWRITE (post-Codex-audit; supersedes the 2026-07-09 line above per last-line-wins). f7's survivor arms are OUTCOME-SELECTED (they survive f4/f6 verdicts), so RC-8 of the reuse ruling governs their already-seen R1–R3 cells: those cells may enter f7's confirmatory slope/capstone primary ONLY with fresh lower-rung reruns or pre-specified selection-adjusted inference frozen in the pinned analysis; otherwise the slope component is permanently exploratory. There is NO default reuse accounting: the gate-t5 dry-plan MUST present BOTH accountings — full lower-rung re-run AND reuse-adjusted with its evidential discount stated — for explicit maintainer decision, and the RC-5 fresh overlap cell at R3 is required under any accounting that mixes logged rows into any analysis.
rationale: The cross-vendor design audit (registry/audits/resource-optimization-plan/1-design-audit-codex.json) found the prior line's default — consuming survivor lower-rung cells as Case-B comparators inside a future confirmatory slope fit — a CRITICAL loophole (already-seen outcome-selected data determining a confirmatory primary) and found load_bearing:false unsound because the accounting choice is evidentially load-bearing for the capstone. Rewritten per audit amendments 2 and 6; machine-enforced by ERR_P2_REUSE_SURVIVOR (kot_common.check_record_reuse) and the kot-reg/2 outcome_selected_arms + selection_adjusted_inference fields. Revisit condition: the gate-t5 decision itself; a failed RC-5 overlap bound voids any reuse accounting and forces fresh lower-rung runs. Registered by the Fable Designer role (bead kernel-of-truth-utq, revision-1).

claim: REVISION-1 REWRITE (post-Codex-audit; supersedes the 2026-07-09 line above per last-line-wins). Folding reuse-maximising ordering INTO the frozen backlog score encodes the reuse priority without a second citable prioritisation surface, PROVIDED the derived L is validated against STRUCTURED registry edges — non-CLOSED registry/experiments/*.json depends_on + pins.corpus_hashes names and registry/ideas.jsonl requires/dependencies edges — rather than the free-text consumers lists in registry/components.jsonl, which are narrative and gameable. `tools/registry/reuse-check.py lscore` is the derivation + mismatch report; a Fable scorer override of a derived L requires a written justification committed with the stub (GZ-4), so overrides are audit-visible.
rationale: The cross-vendor design audit found free-text consumers gameable as an L input (amendment 6). The structured derivation is deterministic and mechanically checkable; known residual: several non-corpus components (engines/harnesses) currently have free-text-only edges — lscore flags these as MISMATCH rather than silently scoring them, and the structured edge set grows as records/ideas declare dependencies. Revisit condition unchanged: the first generation boundary after the D3 backlog scorer lands — if re-scoring fails to reproduce the plan's section 4.2 wave ordering, the discrepancy is a recorded finding (companion to fork ENG-2). Registered by the Fable Designer role (bead kernel-of-truth-utq, revision-1).

claim: The a5-llm prompt pack (preamble, toy-world few-shot examples, 8 query-render templates), record->English renderer, refusal-marker regexes, and answer normaliser are a stipulated pro-LLM-lenient instrument: every scoring asymmetry (lenient set/boolean match, any-refusal on controls, no provenance requirement) runs in the LLM arms' favour, so engine-superiority readings are conservative. Results are indexed to this instrument identity; changing any element post-freeze is an instrument version change requiring a correction record.
resolution_path: Pre-freeze toy-world probe pilot (30 synthetic queries, never a5-eval items) decides FK-A5L-1; the in-run extraction gate (Wilson-LB >= 0.90 per cell) is the mechanical check; the Codex audit reviews the leniency direction.
notes: a5-llm design premise. Fairness asymmetries enumerated in the design note section 3.4/3.5.
rationale: The programme proceeds on this stipulated pro-LLM-lenient instrument because every scoring asymmetry runs in the LLM arms' favour, so any engine-superiority reading it yields is conservative and safe to act on.

claim: The a5-llm record->text rendering plus rendered axiom block is a faithful kernel-as-text (Law-2) form of the same content the engine consumes, and the exact-slug-complete deterministic retrieval makes the llm-rag arm an ORACLE-STRONG null that upper-bounds deployable RAG on this slice.
resolution_path: Build-time retrieval-completeness lint (expected-answer support records subset-of retrieved set, /analysis/retrieval_completeness_violations = 0) mechanically verifies completeness; faithfulness of the rendering is reviewed at the Codex audit.
notes: a5-llm design premise; the differentiator claim is earned against this strongest-form null or not at all.
rationale: The programme proceeds because the rendered record is a faithful Law-2 kernel-as-text form and the exact-slug-complete retrieval upper-bounds deployable RAG, so a differentiator earned against this arm is earned against the strongest available null.

claim: The pinned SmolLM2-Instruct checkpoint revisions (2024) predate the 2026-07-09 code-corpus-v0 snapshot of this repo's own tooling, so the llm-direct arms are closed-book on the evaluated facts by construction; their honest role is the fabrication/refusal surface plus the scale sign, never a knowledge claim.
resolution_path: Not directly verifiable (training-set membership); the dated-artifact argument stands unless a checkpoint revision is ever un-pinned or the snapshot provenance changes — either voids this stipulation.
notes: a5-llm design premise; disclosed in the envelope (direct arms measure fabrication/refusal, not knowledge).
rationale: The programme proceeds because the pinned pre-snapshot checkpoints make the direct-LLM arms closed-book on the evaluated facts by construction, so their honest role is the fabrication/refusal surface and the scale sign, never a knowledge claim.

claim: The kot-query/1 define-op is the closed two-shape operator DEFINE (retrieve a concept's endorsed genus-differentia definition at one level, verbatim) plus DEFINE-MATCH (exact structural set-equality of a candidate definition against it); there is no third shape and any addition is a grammar-version change.
rationale: The programme proceeds on the two-shape surface because DEFINE + DEFINE-MATCH is the minimal grammar that makes definitional items checkable; the stipulation falls only if a third definitional question-class is found unrepresentable.

claim: In DEFINE-MATCH a licensed definitive false is sound (unlike lookup/instance which refuse on absence) because a logicalDefinition is a closed, complete object about its own definiendum, so 'candidate != X's endorsed definition' is a licensed negative, not a CWA incompleteness.
rationale: The programme proceeds because definitional closure — the endorsed definition is the whole, complete stipulation about X — licenses a definitive negative that makes def-MMLU distractor options checkable; the cross-vendor audit is the check and if false-licensing proves unsound the op reverts to DEFINE-only.
notes: define-op design premise (memo ASM-DEF-2); its audit is the §7.4 kill check.

claim: The definitional licence is a stratum-3, corpus-scoped 'definitional' endorsement record admitting a pinned onto-obo shard's logicalDefinitions (by shard name + sourceVersion sha) as a definitional-source, with an optional per-concept override retained.
rationale: The programme proceeds on corpus-scoped granularity because a shard shares one provenance, semanticStatus, licence and extraction sha, so a single shard-scoped endorsement keeps the governance act legible and cheap while the per-concept override still allows dis-endorsing a contested definition.

claim: 'definitional' is a new kot-axiom/1 endorsement-kind, disjoint from the extension-constraint kinds {functional, cardinality, disjointWith, inverseOf, domain, range}; it is validated and consumed only by the define-op and never enters the CWA store-validation pass over world-layer facts.
rationale: The programme proceeds on the disjoint endorsement-kind because keeping the definitional endorsement (stratum-3 governance) out of the world-layer CWA pass preserves strata separation: definitions stay in identity/hash, endorsements stay a sidecar governance act.

claim: The WiC / definitional-MMLU kappa_B yield of the define-op is a projection, not a measured quantity: the substrate is biomedical (GO/PO) and WiC is general-vocabulary, so the define-checkable yield may be small or ~0.
resolution_path: The b-cov census re-run (docs/design-kot-query-define-op.md §7.2 / docs/next/architecture-ladder.md §10.1, Tier 0, ~$0, CPU-only) measures kappa_B^engine for gold-parse and mapper-parse before/after the op lands; the measured delta converts this projection. NEVER a premise for building the op or any downstream claim.
notes: define-op EXTRAPOLATION (memo ASM-DEF-5); non-load-bearing, resolved by the census.

claim: The define mapper leg is a closed pinned template inventory plus an onto-obo annotation-label->URN index reusing the mapper's abstain-on-ambiguity discipline (exactly-one-URN maps; more-than-one abstains; zero unmapped); it resolves no word sense and no ambiguous label, so a mapper-parse is emitted only when TERM/GENUS/REL/FILLER each resolve to exactly one URN.
rationale: The programme proceeds on closed-templates + abstain-on-ambiguity because it reuses the mapper's existing verified discipline so no sense or ambiguous label is silently resolved, and the census prices the gold-parse-vs-mapper-parse loss separately rather than the design guessing it.
